name: Internal user-targeted expense migration
description: Add a server-only expense migration path so maintainers can import one legacy JSON file into an explicit existing user account without using that user's JWT. The new `POST /api/v1/internal/migrate/expenses` route is protected by `X-Internal-Api-Key` matched against Worker env secret `INTERNAL_API_KEY`, requires `targetUserId`, validates that the target user exists, reuses the target user's household membership/role checks for household imports, and persists imported expenses with `spentByUserId` set to the target user. The existing authenticated `POST /api/v1/migrate/expenses` route remains unchanged and still binds imports to the caller's own token identity. The CLI now supports mutually exclusive token mode or internal mode (`--admin-secret` + `--target-user-id`) without printing secrets.

planned: Add an additive internal migration endpoint and CLI mode for one-file-per-user operator imports without weakening the existing self-token route.
done: `apps/worker/src/middlewares/internal-auth.ts` adds internal secret middleware that checks `X-Internal-Api-Key` against `INTERNAL_API_KEY` with constant-time comparison and returns 401 on missing/wrong secret.

`apps/worker/src/routes/migrate.ts` now exposes both the existing user-authenticated `POST /api/v1/migrate/expenses` path and additive internal `POST /api/v1/internal/migrate/expenses` path with separate middleware boundaries.

`apps/worker/src/handlers/migrate/migrate-expenses.ts` refactors the import flow around shared migration logic so the public route still uses `currentUser.id` while the internal route validates `targetUserId`, resolves the target user, and reuses target-user household authorization before writes.

`apps/worker/src/contracts/migrate-schemas.ts` and `apps/worker/src/contracts/migrate-types.ts` keep the public schema strict and unchanged while adding a separate internal request contract that requires `targetUserId`.
`apps/worker/src/lib/env.ts`, `apps/worker/src/types/app.ts`, `apps/worker/.dev.vars.example`, and `apps/worker/vitest.config.mts` add the new internal secret config for runtime and tests.
`scripts/migrate-expenses.mjs` now supports internal mode via `--admin-secret` + `--target-user-id`, rejects invalid flag combinations, and keeps token mode unchanged without printing secrets.

`apps/worker/test/integration/migrate-expenses-test-setup.ts`, `apps/worker/test/integration/migrate-expenses.spec.ts`, and `apps/worker/test/integration/migrate-expenses-internal.spec.ts` cover personal import, household import, missing/wrong secret, missing/unknown target user, household membership denial, public-route isolation from internal auth headers, and the split public/internal route coverage introduced during PR prep.

The follow-up mapping fix restored legacy category coverage for `7 -> repairs` and `21 -> self-development` in `apps/worker/src/handlers/migrate/shared.ts` and added regression tests so those ids no longer fall through as unknown categories.
`docs/exec-plans/plans/2026-07-06-feat-124-internal-user-targeted-migrate.md` and `docs/exec-plans/index.md` were updated through completion, including resolved decisions and closeout notes.
verification: `pnpm --filter worker cf-typegen` completed without error and produced no `apps/worker/worker-configuration.d.ts` diff because `INTERNAL_API_KEY` is a secret, not a generated var binding.
`pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/integration/migrate-expenses.spec.ts` passed with 22/22 tests green.

`pnpm --filter worker exec vitest run test/integration/migrate-expenses.spec.ts test/integration/migrate-expenses-internal.spec.ts` passed with 24/24 tests green after restoring legacy mappings `7 -> repairs` and `21 -> self-development`.
`@oracle` implementation review approved the diff with no blocking issues; only harness/plan closeout remained.

notes: The new internal route is intentionally operator-only and does not add general staff/admin RBAC. The public migrate endpoint still never accepts target-user override. Dry-run remains the recommended first step and real imports remain non-idempotent, so rerunning the same file can still create duplicates.
